channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Marsh adquiere las acciones restantes de Asterra
item.description: Marsh acaba de anunciar la adquisición del 60% restante de las acciones que no poseía en Asterra, confirmando la noticia publicada esta mañana por el BDS. Los términos de la transacción, que ha recibido la aprobación de los organismos reguladores, no han sido divulgados.

item.title: Viewpoint: Boom in Hyperscale Data Centers Puts Insurers to the Test
item.description: Hyperscale data center campuses represent the latest growth opportunity for global insurers and reinsurers. On top of that, new premiums from hyperscale data centers are expected to reach $10 billion annually this year, dwarfing the global aviation insurance market’s $5 billion in annual premiums. Annual investment in these specialized facilities will likely surpass $300 billion by 2027, and the total insurable asset base for the roughly 11,000 data centers currently in operation already exceeds $2 trillion.

item.title: Illinois Could See Highest Catastrophe Claim Volume in a Decade: State Farm
item.description: Severe weather activity is putting Illinois on pace for the highest catastrophe claim volume in a decade, according to an analysis by State Farm. Homeowner claims are currently 47% above the 10-year average, and State Farm Auto is on track for its second-highest catastrophe claim volume in the past 10 years, with claims 101% above the long-term average. As of June 19, State Farm customers in Illinois have filed nearly 50,000 severe-weather-related claims for home and auto.

item.title: Executives on the Move at AIG, HDI, Everest Group and The Andover Cos.
item.description: American International Group (AIG) announced that Nancy Bewlay has been named executive vice president, global chief underwriting officer, effective September 8, 2026. With more than 30 years’ experience, Bewlay joins AIG from AXA Group, where she served as group chief underwriting and pricing officer and a member of the Group Management Committee since 2023. announced two key leadership appointments within Everest Global Wholesale & Specialty (GWS), effective October 1.

item.title: Australia’s cyclone reinsurance pool only part of the answer, brokers association says
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Payments Vision Delivery Committee update on future retail payments infrastructure
item.description: The Retail Payments Infrastructure Board (RPIB), led by the Bank of England, recently published a consultation on the future retail payments infrastructure. To support the consultation, the Payment Vision Delivery Committee (PVDC) which comprises representatives of HM Treasury, the FCA, Bank of England and the PSR, has published further context to support stakeholders' reading of the consultation. Both the PVDC and RPIB were set up to deliver the government’s National Payments Vision of a trusted, world-leading payments ecosystem delivered on next generation technology, where consumers and businesses have a choice of payment methods to meet their needs.

item.title: Pension firms must do more for customers in older pensions and fund savings
item.description: The regulator identified some good practices, but complex charging structures, older product design and weakness in firms' data meant some pension savers are not getting as much value as they could. There was evidence of firms capping or reducing charges for customers in legacy products. The regulator is also engaging with firms on barriers they face in improving the value for customers, particularly in closed books.

item.title: Motor finance scheme partially suspended
item.description: The Upper Tribunal has confirmed it will hear the legal challenges to our motor finance scheme on 14 to 18 December 2026 or 16 to 26 February 2027. The Tribunal has also made an order suspending parts of the scheme on terms agreed by us with the 4 commercial parties that have challenged (Consumer Voice, represented by Courmacs Legal, Volkswagen Financial Services, Mercedes Benz Financial Services, and Crédit Agricole Auto Finance). The Upper Tribunal has made an order suspending parts of the scheme.

item.title: Crunchbase Data: Global Startup Investment Hit Record $510B In H1 2026 As AI Boom Accelerates Funding And Exits
item.description: Global venture funding reached a record $510 billion in the first half of 2026, surpassing the $440 billion invested in all of 2025 and setting a new high for startup investment in any half-year period on record, Crunchbase data shows. IPOs and startup acquisitions accelerated alongside venture investment, producing the strongest exit market since the 2021 boom, Crunchbase data shows. At the same time, other massive funding deals across industries including AI infrastructure, defense, robotics and healthcare — combined with record IPO and M&A activity — signal that the AI investment boom has grown well beyond a select few top foundation labs.

item.title: Economic losses from Venezuela quakes to exceed $10bn, insured impact still unknown: Verisk
item.description: The Catastrophe and Risk Solutions group at Verisk has estimated that economic losses from the June 24, 2026 earthquakes in Venezuela will likely exceed USD 10 billion, with a higher degree of uncertainty than usual in estimating the insured share of industry losses due to Venezuela’s macroeconomic conditions, elevated inflation, low insurance penetration, and sanctions-related market complexities. “The modeled insured loss estimates do not include losses resulting from fire-following, landslides, sprinkler leakage, loss adjustment expenses, damage to uninsured properties or infrastructure, extra-contractual obligations, hazardous waste cleanup, vandalism, or civil commotion, whether directly or indirectly caused by the event,” Verisk commented. Verisk’s estimate sits in line with the US Geological Survey’s (USGS), who previously said that there was a 45% chance the economic costs from the event could exceed USD 10 billion.

item.title: The Fidelis Partnership’s debut Woody Re cat bond an “important step” as syndicate grows: Woods
item.description: – Woody Re 2026-1 catastrophe bond represents an “important step” for The Fidelis Partnership in building out reinsurance capacity to support its Syndicate 3123 at Lloyd’s as it grows, according to David Woods, Head of Portfolio & Exposure and Ireland Chief Executive Officer. The cat bond demonstrates a “disciplined approach to capital management,” complementing its traditional reinsurance with capacity from the capital markets via catastrophe bond investors, and now being part of a strategy to “build a diversified and resilient protection programme to support its continued growth.” Commenting on the successful debut cat bond David Woods, Head of Portfolio & Exposure and Ireland Chief Executive Officer at The Fidelis Partnership, said, “Securing this cat bond protection for Syndicate 3123 is an important step in building out a diversified, resilient reinsurance programme as the Syndicate continues to grow. The now issued $75 million of Woody Re 2026-1 Class A cat bond notes provide the Fidelis Partnership linked Syndicate 3123 at Lloyd’s with a source of catastrophe reinsurance against losses from named storms, earthquakes, severe thunderstorms, winter storms and wildfire events in part of North America on an annual aggregate, industry loss index trigger basis, running across a three year term to the end of June 2029.

item.title: Malibu Life establishes US retail annuity origination platform with TruSpire acquisition
item.description: retirement and annuity market, has completed its acquisition of TruSpire Retirement Insurance Company, which will be rebranded as Malibu Life USA. TruSpire (now Malibu Life USA) is a U.S.-domiciled life and retirement insurance company focused on the retail annuity market. origination capabilities with Malibu Life’s capital management discipline and Third Point’s institutional credit investment platform, the company is well positioned to build a differentiated annuity franchise focused on attractive spread income, prudent risk management and long-term book value.

item.title: Florida insurers secure additional $5-$7bn reinsurance coverage at mid-year renewals: Aon
item.description: Florida’s insurers entered mid-year renewals from a position of strength, securing an additional $5-$7 billion of reinsurance coverage as ample supply was more than adequate to meet increased demand, according to Aon’s recent Reinsurance Market Dynamics report. As the market in the US state stabilises, Florida insurers purchased an additional $5 billion to $7 billion in reinsurance coverage during the mid-year renewals, driven by further depopulation of Florida’s state-backed Citizens Property Insurance Corporation. In addition to a stronger balance sheet, Florida insurers began the mid-year renewals with improved underwriting results, marked by the recent legal reforms to address litigation and underwriting actions taken by insurers.

item.title: Bishop appointed as Partner Re’s CUO, Global Cat
item.description: Olivia Bishop has been appointed Chief Underwriting Officer, Global Catastrophe, at Bermuda-based reinsurer Partner Re, a role she will hold alongside her current position as SVP, Head of North America Catastrophe. Adding: “I’m grateful for the opportunity to contribute more broadly across our global catastrophe platform while continuing to lead our North America business.” The executive has been with Partner Re for 16 years, having joined the company in 2010 as an AVP – Underwriter. “As the catastrophe landscape evolves, I’m excited to help drive our underwriting strategy and deliver value to our clients and partners,” Bishop commented.

item.title: Germany Charges Former Ukrainian Army Officer With Nord Stream Pipeline Attack
item.description: German federal prosecutors have charged a former Ukrainian army officer with being the co-perpetrator of a war crime over the 2022 Nord Stream gas pipeline blasts, accusing him of acting on behalf of Ukrainian state entities. The explosions damaged the Nord Stream 1 pipeline, a vital route for Russian gas exports to Europe, as well as the Nord Stream 2 branch, which had yet to enter service. Read more : Nord Stream Blasts Due to War, Say Insurers Seeking to Avoid Pay Out Authorities in Kyiv said on Thursday that they did not have enough information about the case to respond in detail to German prosecutors’ allegations.

item.title: Bayer Fuels Break-Up Talk as Roundup Business Put Into Separate Unit
item.description: Roundup business in a new unit, less than a week after s coring a major legal victory that blocked thousands of state-court lawsuits related to the weedkiller. glyphosate business separately would help Bayer “separate or divest the business in the future should ongoing or additional litigation make the business … unattractive.” Bayer said Ruveon will remain a Bayer business and oversee all aspects of U.S. Bayer bought Roundup as part of its $63 billion purchase of agrochemical company Monsanto in 2018, which became a major liability due to lawsuits accusing Bayer of failing to warn users that Roundup’s active ingredient causes cancer.

item.title: Flood Re to Cut Insurance Payouts to Richest UK Households
item.description: The UK’s flood insurer of last resort is unfairly favoring wealthier households and will be reformed to reduce the burden of multimillion-pound payouts, the government said Wednesday. Separately, Flood Re and the insurance industry will agree to cap the value of claims that can be passed on to the program, while Flood Re will also revise the premiums it charges insurers to better balance between wealthier and poorer households and areas, Hardy said. Some of those properties in Band H, the highest band, are now worth millions of pounds and insurance payouts for those claims are disproportionately high, Flood Re Chief Executive Officer Perry Thomas said in March.

item.title: Allianz UK launches car insurance brand Slick Cover
item.description: Allianz UK has launched Slick Cover , a new digital-first car insurance brand created as a managing general agent (MGA) to develop and test new insurance propositions more quickly. According to Allianz, Slick Cover went from concept to selling its first motor policy in less than six months. “We’re very excited to launch Slick into the UK personal lines market and provide more choice to customers who are looking for great value cover for their car, and want quick access to their insurance information.

item.title: Admiral Business partners with ANNA Money
item.description: Admiral Business has partnered with ANNA Money to offer business insurance through the fintech’s platform, embedding insurance into key moments in the SME customer journey. The partnership will introduce Admiral Business’ insurance products to ANNA Money’s more than 100,000 customers during events such as company formation and early business growth, aiming to address underinsurance among small businesses. By working with ANNA Money, we’re supporting customers where they are, making it easier for small business owners to put the right protection in place and focus on what matters most: running their business.” – Emma Huntington, CEO of Admiral Pioneer.

item.title: Hagerty to acquire Bennetts
item.description: Hagerty announced that it has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Bennetts, the United Kingdom’s #2 specialty motorcycle insurance broker, from Lucida Group for £34 million ($43 million). Founded more than 90 years ago, Bennetts brings 15% UK motorcycle insurance market share and a 65 Net Promoter Score through a member-centric approach similar to Hagerty’s model in the enthusiast car space. Bennetts’ book comprises 92% enthusiast riders and has a risk profile that closely mirrors Hagerty’s enthusiast car insurance portfolio.

item.title: AssuranceAmerica Suffers Third-Party Data Breach, Customer Data Exposed
item.description: It was determined an unauthorized third party accessed the company’s systems through the targeted attack and copied “a number of data files,” according to the breach notice filed in at least a half-dozen states. Related: NAIC Says Data Taken in Hack Has Been Published Online AssuranceAmerica said that, due to the scope and the files involved, the investigation was only recently completed. About three months after it detected suspicious activity within its network systems, AssuranceAmerica has started alerting consumers.

item.title: US Decides Against Renewing USMCA, Shifting to Rolling Talks
item.description: The U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement, or USMCA, will remain in force for another decade provided no one country decides to exit. Opting against a longer-term renewal opens the door to years of contentious negotiations over the rules governing continent-wide supply chains and low tariff levels vital for automakers, farmers, retailers and energy companies. “We think there are substantial issues.” The decision marked a head-spinning reversal for President Donald Trump, who had rammed through the original USMCA in 2020 and once called it the “best and most important trade deal ever made.” Trump soured on the deal in his second term in part because it protects huge sections of trade from tariffs he sought to impose and did little to address trade deficits with Mexico and Canada.

item.title: Changes in Policy Language, Provisions Suppressing Claim Volume, Report Shows
item.description: Changes in policy language and policy provisions are a big factor in suppressing the volume, authors of Verisk’s property report on the first quarter say. claim volume fell 8.9% from last year, with total claims dipping 13.1% below the five-year average, despite several large catastrophe events, a new report shows. residential roof replacement costs jumped 33% and repair costs rose 25% in 2025 compared to the prior four-year average despite a sharp decline in claims volume, a report out from Verisk in June shows.

item.title: Agent-Carrier Relationships Improving, Survey Shows
item.description: Or maybe deliberate carrier investments in agency relationships are paying off. First Connect Chief Executive Officer Aviad Pinkovezky tossed out the two possibilities to explain the markedly improved picture of agents’ ability to find capacity, understand carrier appetites, and receive timely quotes this year in an introduction to First Connect’s “ 2026 State of the Industry Report .” According to the report, published in late June: Contrast that to First Connect’s prior “ 2025 State of the Industry Report ,” when 86% of agents said they had faced product availability challenges—with 44% reporting “significant” availability challenges—and 17% reporting “significant” problems with carrier appetite clarity. Related 2025 article : Agents Struggle to Find Capacity, Meet Customer Timing Expectations: Survey “These are not marginal shifts.

item.title: EBM Insurance & Risk hands Ryan Cameron, Ashton George expanded leadership roles
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Reinsurance pools must be designed ‘absorb volatility, not just price for it’: ARPC chief
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Coface taps Ivor Miric as head of debt collection sales for APAC
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Financial regulator to simplify investment disclosure regime
item.description: The move will bring all investment cost disclosures into line with previous investment product disclosure reforms and create a more consistent framework for firms to give customers clearer, more useful information. 'The changes will give firms more freedom to innovate and communicate in ways that build trust and support informed decisions to help consumers navigate their financial lives.' The changes are part of the FCA’s wider work to support growth by creating a consumer investment market that is resilient, competitive and better for firms and customers. The FCA is proposing to simplify how platforms, advisers and wealth managers communicate the costs of investing while reminding firms to communicate with consumers about investing in plain English.

item.title: California utility LADWP targets low-end pricing for $100m 123 Lights Re wildfire cat bond
item.description: The Los Angeles Department of Water and Power (LADWP) is aiming to price its fourth catastrophe bond sponsorship at the low-end of initial guidance, while still seeking $100 million of California wildfire protection from the 123 Lights Re Ltd. California utility the Los Angeles Department of Water and Power (LADWP) returned to the catastrophe bond market in June, aiming to secure another $100 million of multi-year wildfire insurance protection from the capital markets . 123 Lights Re Ltd., a Bermuda special purpose insurer (SPI), continues to offer investors a single $100 million tranche of Series 2026-1 notes that will cascade down insurance coverage to the LADWP, which will provide the utility protection on a county-weighted industry loss index basis covering wildfire events in the state of California over a roughly three year term until the end of August 2029.

item.title: Swiss Re sets top Matterhorn Re 2026-3 retro cat bond target of $345m as pricing falls twice
item.description: Swiss Re is a strategic retro buyer through the Matterhorn Re cat bond program, in the past having secured a range of coverage types to hedge peak natural catastrophe exposures and even having used the structure for mortality and cyber retrocession as well. This new Matterhorn Re Series 2026-3 catastrophe bond sees Swiss Re aiming to secure broad-based North American peak peril retrocession, with five tranches of notes on offer to provide a range of industry-loss index based coverages across aggregate and occurrence limits for earthquake and named storm perils in the US and Canada, with different terms for some tranches. (Series 2026-3) catastrophe bond offering, while the price guidance for the notes has now been lowered twice, Artemis can report.

item.title: Cat bond maturities and coupons fuel capital, issuance to exceed $20bn again: Aon Securities
item.description: Catastrophe bond issuance is expected to exceed $20 billion for the second year in a row in 2026, according to Aon Securities, with the maturity schedule seen as a “constructive” market feature that when added to coupon earnings means cash and capital levels for fund managers and investors have been particularly high this year. With first-half issuance of catastrophe bonds already having set a new record, as we detailed in our brand new quarterly report this morning, Aon Securities is bullish on the potential for $20 billion to be eclipsed again. The investment banking and ILS unit of Aon explains that expansion of the ILS market’s capital base has been “predominantly driven by notable 144A catastrophe bond issuances.” “Pricing remains attractive for catastrophe bonds, supporting strong issuance volume and record outstanding capacity,” the company said.

item.title: SiriusPoint names Mark Aspinall as Head of Fine Art & Specie
item.description: SiriusPoint, a specialty insurer and reinsurer based in Bermuda, has appointed Mark Aspinall as Head of its new Fine Art & Specie team, effective January 2027. “Mark’s deep technical expertise and strong broker relationships will be critical as we establish a scalable Fine Art & Specie offering within our specialty platform, which complements adjacent Marine classes like cargo and war, political violence and terrorism.” Aspinall’s appointment follows that of Ed Winter and Paul Beattie, who lead the reinsurer’s new Crisis Solutions offering, and Ella Stevenson, Crisis Solutions Underwriter. In his new role, Aspinall will be based in London and report to Chris Fenn, Global Head of Marine, SiriusPoint.

item.title: EMEA reinsurance market experiences continued softening at July renewals: Gallagher Re
item.description: Reinsurance buyers in the Middle East secured significant risk-adjusted rate reductions during the July 2026 renewals, as abundant capacity and strong competition accelerate a softening market across the EMEA region. The Gallagher Re July 2026 First View renewal report reveals that reinsurance buyers reached a period where conditions shifted further in their favour, allowing cedents to secure risk-adjusted rate reductions across numerous geographies and classes. This was also the case in the EMEA, where cedants successfully leveraged the region’s conditions to obtain better terms and conditions from reinsurers with increased appetite.

item.title: TMK appoints Alex Barnes as Departmental Head of Marine & Energy
item.description: Tokio Marine Kiln (TMK), an international specialty insurer, has announced the appointment of Alex Barnes as Departmental Head of Marine & Energy. Since January 2026, Barnes has led the Marine & Energy department on an interim basis and now takes up the role permanently with immediate effect. Vivek Syal, Chief Underwriting Officer at TMK, said, “Alex brings deep technical expertise and a strong track record, and her appointment reflects our commitment to building leading market positions across our Marine & Energy underwriting portfolio.

item.title: Travelers Builds Insurance-Specific LLM
item.description: According to Travelers, the proprietary LLM outperformed commercially available AI models in tests seeking answers to “tens of thousands” of insurance-related questions. She stressed that while frontier AI models are advancing quickly, for the insurer, “true competitive advantage” comes from combining capabilities of frontier AI models “with what only Travelers has: decades of institutional knowledge, millions of proprietary documents, deep insurance expertise, and world-class engineering.” “AI is only as valuable as the judgment behind it,” she wrote on LinkedIn. “That’s why we’re combining leading AI capabilities with Travelers’ unique expertise, all grounded in responsible AI practices.” In the media statement, Lefebvre added that instant accessibility to Travelers institutional data, experience and expertise across the enterprise, allows Travelers professionals to “act more quickly and effectively, delivering even greater value to our customers and distribution partners.” Get the insurance industry's trusted newsletter

item.title: People Moves: Miller Appoints Plant Head of North American Property
item.description: Miller , an independent specialist (re)insurance broker headquartered in London, appointed Kim Plant as head of North American property. Plant joins Miller from Amwins, where he served as a director in its North American property division from 2020, where he formed relationships across the North American Property market in both the U.S. At Miller, Kim will be responsible for leading the business’s North American property proposition out of London and Bermuda, working closely with Matthew Keeping, Miller’s managing director, head of property and casualty.

item.title: Tech and Finance Sectors Losing 28,000 Jobs Monthly Show AI Impact on Labor
item.description: The weakness stands out against an otherwise robust labor market that created more than 113,000 jobs monthly this year through May — a number that would have been much higher if the banking and tech industries hadn’t dragged down the overall total. Research suggests AI’s impact on the labor market depends on how companies deploy the technology. A decline in payrolls in the financial-activities and information sectors — where AI adoption rates have been fastest — has accelerated in 2026, to 28,000 per month on average based on government data.

item.title: Continuum hires Howden’s Andy Tam as Hong Kong general manager
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: We’ve seen reinsurers’ appetite prevail at 1.7, Gallagher Re’s Roshan Perera says
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: A Troubling Communication Gap on Annuities
item.description: Annuities have long been a versatile financial tool for this demographic, providing predictable income and financial security at a time when heightened concern for stability is warranted. When communication fails, it creates inefficiency, with 46% of consumers contacting customer support teams when communications are difficult to understand. Despite surging annuity demand, half of consumers struggle to understand communications from insurers, creating costly service gaps and eroding trust.

item.title: Agentic AI Must Prioritize Decision Velocity
item.description: Decision velocity is truly what agentic AI in insurance must aim for. Agentic AI's true value in insurance lies not in speed alone, but in decision velocity with built-in governance and accountability. Decision velocity brings this discipline to speed and scale.

item.title: King Risk Partners acquires Connecticut construction insurance specialist
item.description: King Risk Partners has acquired Roberts Agency , an independent insurance agency based in Bloomfield, Connecticut, expanding its presence in the state and adding expertise in construction insurance and surety bonding. Headquartered in Gainesville, Florida, King Risk Partners is the 51st-largest insurance brokerage in the U.S. The acquisition expands King Risk Partners’ capabilities in the Northeast while adding to its commercial and employee benefits offerings.

item.title: World Insurance Associates acquires ML Ruberton Agency and MLR Risk Management
item.description: World Insurance Associates has acquired the business of ML Ruberton Agency and MLR Risk Management, both based in Hammonton, New Jersey. MLR provides personal and commercial property insurance, life and health insurance, as well as consulting services. It’s great to join World and expand the products and services we provide to our customers.” “I’d like to extend a warm welcome to MLR as they join the World family,” said Rich Eknoian, executive chairman and founder of World.

item.title: Venezuela Earthquake Death Toll Nears 2,000
item.description: The death toll from the twin earthquakes that struck Venezuela last week rose to nearly 2,000, as aftershocks eased in both frequency and intensity and authorities expanded recovery efforts. Almost 20,000 people survived the disaster in the hardest-hit coastal state of La Guaira, National Assembly President Jorge Rodríguez said Tuesday during a televised briefing. The earthquakes left more than 10,000 people injured and 15,000 displaced, he added.

item.title: Wayve Courts Automakers With AI Driving System That Learns Like Humans
item.description: Wayve uses an artificial-intelligence technology called end-to-end machine learning to navigate roadways, which is supposed to instantly translate sensor-generated data into driving decisions, much like a human driver. Wayve engineers believe the conventional, programming-intensive safety approach hinders an AI driving system’s ability to stay safe in unusual cases because it is hard to write rules to prepare for very unusual situations. Autonomous-driving startup Wayve is riding a tide of investor interest.

item.title: July 4 Holiday Cargo Thieves Are Ready Now, Report Warns
item.description: Cargo thieves may be operating sooner than you think to take advantage of the July 4 holiday, a new report shows. Verisk CargoNet is warning transportation and logistics stakeholders to prepare for elevated cargo theft risk during the July 4 holiday, when reduced staffing, closures and staged freight can create opportunities for organized crime. The predictable disruption in the supply chain leads Cargo thieves to understand when freight is likely to be parked, when facilities may be closed and when normal verification procedures may be loser, the report’s authors say.

item.title: Recreational Marijuana Can Be Sold in Virginia Retail Stores Beginning in 2027
item.description: “This compromise gives us a smarter and safer path forward — one that protects consumers, keeps products tested and accurately labeled, and creates a legal marketplace that is affordable and accessible enough to actually compete.” Here’s what to know about Virginia’s new law, the long process of enabling retail sales and how the state’s changes fit into the national picture: Virginia’s law allows new stores, higher possession limit Virginia already had a medical marijuana program that allowed patients to purchase the drug through dispensaries. Five years after becoming the first Southern state to legalize possession of marijuana, Virginia has approved a legal way to sell it to recreational users. State budget legislation enacted Monday will allow up to 350 cannabis shops to open across Virginia beginning July 1, 2027.

item.title: NY Man Sentenced for Fencing Organized Crime Ring’s $4.4M Jewelry Theft
item.description: According to court documents and statements made in court, between May 2023 and April 2024, an organized jewelry theft ring of Colombian nationals burglarized jewelry stores and kiosks in malls in Connecticut, Illinois, Indiana, New Jersey, Ohio, and Virginia. A Brooklyn, New York, man was sentenced last week for fencing stolen jewelry taken from stores and kiosks across the country, according to the United States Attorney’s Office for the District of Columbia. The man, sentenced to serve 24 months in prison and three years of supervised release, knowingly bought and sold jewelry stolen from mall-based stores and kiosks across the country.

item.title: Ford Recall Covers 741,000 U.S. Vehicles Due to Potential Park System Defect
item.description: over a transmission defect that can damage the park system, which in turn could result in vehicles rolling away, the U.S. Ford is recalling 741,195 vehicles in the U.S. The recall affects certain 2018-2021 Navigator, Expedition, 2020-2021 Explorer, Lincoln Aviator, and 2021 F-150 vehicles, the regulator said.

item.title: Record reinsurance capital drives creative solutions at mid-year renewals: Aon
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles
